info.title: netctl control-plane API
info.description: Operational endpoints plus the versioned /v1 resource API. All /v1 routes are tenant-scoped; in S9 the tenant is resolved by a dev stub (default tenant, optional X-Netctl-Tenant override) — real identity (SSO/SCIM, per-tenant IdP) lands in S18.

tags.name: agents
tags.description: Registered agents (created via mTLS registration; managed here).

tags.name: changes
tags.description: Change intelligence: the change timeline + change-to-incident correlation (S29).

get.description: Returns 200 when dependencies (the database) are reachable, otherwise 503.

get.summary: Latest discovered network path for a test (path-viz data)

post.summary: Run a path discovery for a test and return the path

get.summary: Get an incident with its signal timeline

200.description: The incident and its time-ordered signals.

get.summary: Read a page of the tenant's tamper-evident audit trail

parameters.name: after
parameters.description: Return events with seq greater than this cursor.

200.description: A page of audit events plus the next cursor.

get.summary: Verify the integrity of the tenant's audit chain

200.description: The verification result; ok=false with a detail when the chain is broken.

get.summary: The authenticated caller's tenant, identity, and effective permissions
Begin OIDC SSO login (redirects to the tenant's identity provider)

parameters.name: tenant
parameters.description: Tenant UUID to log into; omitted → the default tenant.

302.description: Redirect to the identity provider's authorization endpoint.

get.summary: OIDC redirect URI: completes login and sets the session cookie

302.description: Session established; redirect to the app.

post.summary: Revoke the current session and clear the session cookie
Ask the AI assistant (cited, RBAC-scoped root-cause analysis)

200.description: A cited root-cause answer scoped to the caller's tenant and permissions.

503.description: The AI assistant is temporarily unavailable.

post.summary: Submit feedback (thumbs up/down) on an AI answer
Author a test config from a natural-language request (schema-validated, pending confirmation)

200.description: A schema-valid test proposal — NOT created; apply via POST /v1/tests.

422.description: Could not author a valid config from the request.

post.summary: Propose monitorable targets mined from observed telemetry (ranked, deduped; pending confirmation)

200.description: Ranked discovery proposals — none are created.
The tenant's change events, newest first.

get.summary: List changes correlated to an incident (candidate causes)

200.description: Recent changes correlated to the incident, ranked as candidate causes.

TenantHeader.name: X-Netctl-Tenant
TenantHeader.description: Dev stub (S9): a tenant UUID to scope the request to. Omitted → the default tenant. Replaced by session-resolved identity in S18.

Error.description: The standard error envelope returned by every endpoint.

TestRequest.description: The mutable fields of a synthetic test.

target.description: Required for all types except noop.

HopNode.description: One responder at a TTL; multiple nodes at a TTL are ECMP branches.

secret.description: Webhook HMAC secret; redacted (***) in responses.

Signal.description: One plane's observation on an incident timeline (extensible: plane/kind are free-form, attributes is arbitrary).

Me.description: The authenticated caller: the resolved tenant (always present), identity, and the effective RBAC permission keys within that tenant.

AuditEvent.description: One record in the tenant's hash-chained, tamper-evident audit log.

actor.description: Who performed the action (email or user id).

target.description: The id of the affected resource.

next.description: Cursor to pass as ?after= for the next page (0 when no more).

detail.description: Present when ok=false: where and how the chain is broken.

question.description: Natural-language question, e.g. "why is api.example.com slow?"

subject.description: Optional explicit subject pins (target, prefix, node).

prompt.description: e.g. "check Salesforce login from every branch"

ChangeEvent.description: A normalized change event ingested from a signed webhook (S29).

ChangeCandidate.description: A change scored as a candidate cause of an incident.
